implementationGroups.name: Asset Visibility and Lifecycle Tracking
implementationGroups.description: Continuously track, inventory, and remediate every device connected to the organizational infrastructure — including laptops, servers, IoT endpoints, and network gear — regardless of whether the connection is physical or virtual, so that no asset goes unmonitored.

controls.title: Maintain a Comprehensive Asset Register
controls.description: Create and keep current a detailed catalog of every organizational asset, refreshing it no less than once per quarter.

controls.title: Detect and Remove Unrecognized Devices
controls.description: Operate a repeatable process that identifies and addresses devices appearing on the network without authorization at least once per month.

controls.title: Run Active Network Scans to Find Assets
controls.description: Use probing tools that actively query the network to discover connected devices on a monthly or more frequent cadence.

controls.title: Feed DHCP Logs Into the Asset Inventory
controls.description: Capture lease records from DHCP servers and use them to update the central asset registry automatically.

controls.title: Deploy Passive Asset Discovery Monitoring
controls.description: Instrument the network with passive sensors that identify assets by observing traffic without actively probing hosts.

implementationGroups.name: Software Cataloging and Execution Control
implementationGroups.description: Track every piece of software running across the environment so that only vetted, licensed applications are allowed to execute, and prevent unapproved or unsupported software from being installed or run.

controls.title: Build and Maintain a Software Catalog
controls.description: Compile and regularly refresh an inventory of all software deployed across the organization, updating it no less than every three months.

controls.title: Ensure Only Supported Software Remains Approved
controls.description: Confirm that every application in the approved catalog still receives vendor updates and has not reached end-of-life.

controls.title: Block or Remove Unapproved Applications
controls.description: Use technical enforcement — such as application allowlists — to stop unapproved software from running or uninstall it from endpoints.

controls.title: Automate Software Inventory Collection
controls.description: Deploy tooling that automatically discovers and catalogs software installations across all organizational systems.

controls.title: Enforce an Application Allowlist
controls.description: Configure systems so that only explicitly authorized executables can run, blocking everything else by default.

controls.title: Enforce a Library Allowlist
controls.description: Restrict which shared libraries and code modules may be loaded by allowing only pre-approved entries.

controls.title: Enforce a Script Allowlist
controls.description: Limit script execution to a defined set of approved scripts, preventing arbitrary or untrusted scripts from running.

implementationGroups.name: Information Protection
implementationGroups.description: Implement processes and technical safeguards that protect organizational data in alignment with the enterprise's accepted risk posture, covering classification, access, transit, storage, and disposal.

controls.title: Define a Data Governance Program
controls.description: Create and operate a formal program covering how data is classified, handled, retained, and destroyed throughout its lifecycle.

controls.title: Catalog All Data Assets
controls.description: Build and keep current a registry identifying every significant data store and data set owned by the organization.

controls.title: Apply Need-to-Know Access Restrictions to Data
controls.description: Configure access permissions on data repositories so that users can reach only the information required for their role.

controls.title: Enforce Data Retention Policies
controls.description: Apply retention rules to all data stores so that information is kept only as long as policy or regulation requires.

controls.title: Safely Destroy Data That Is No Longer Needed
controls.description: When data reaches the end of its retention period, dispose of it using methods that prevent recovery.

controls.title: Encrypt Information Stored on User Devices
controls.description: Apply full-disk or file-level encryption to protect data at rest on laptops, desktops, and other endpoint hardware.

controls.title: Operate a Data Classification Scheme
controls.description: Establish criteria and a repeatable workflow for labeling data according to its sensitivity level.

controls.title: Map How Data Moves Through the Organization
controls.description: Document the pathways by which sensitive data flows between systems, applications, and users.

controls.title: Encrypt Portable Storage Media
controls.description: Require encryption on USB drives, external disks, and any other removable media that may carry organizational data.

controls.title: Protect Sensitive Data While in Transit
controls.description: Apply cryptographic protections such as TLS to sensitive information whenever it traverses networks.

controls.title: Protect Sensitive Data at Rest
controls.description: Encrypt or otherwise protect sensitive information stored in databases, file systems, and object storage.

controls.title: Isolate Data Workloads by Sensitivity
controls.description: Place data processing, storage, and maintenance operations into separate environments that match the data's classification level.

controls.title: Deploy Data Leak Prevention Controls
controls.description: Implement monitoring and blocking technology at endpoints, network egress points, and gateways to stop unauthorized data exfiltration.

controls.title: Log Access to Sensitive Information
controls.description: Record who accesses sensitive data, when, and from where to support audit and investigation requirements.

implementationGroups.name: Hardening Assets and Software
implementationGroups.description: Define baseline security settings for all hardware and software, then enforce those baselines across the environment to reduce exploitable misconfigurations.

controls.title: Operate a Configuration Hardening Program
controls.description: Create and follow a documented process for defining, applying, and maintaining secure settings on systems and applications.

controls.title: Apply and Maintain Security Baselines for Assets and Software
controls.description: Use recognized configuration standards to harden every system and application, keeping baselines current over time.

controls.title: Lock Idle Sessions Automatically
controls.description: Configure endpoints and servers to lock their screens or sessions after a period of user inactivity.

controls.title: Run and Manage Host Firewalls on Servers
controls.description: Enable firewall software on every server and maintain rule sets that restrict unnecessary inbound and outbound traffic.

controls.title: Run and Manage Host Firewalls on Endpoints
controls.description: Enable firewall software on user workstations and devices, restricting traffic to only what is required.

controls.title: Apply Ongoing Security Management to Assets and Software
controls.description: Continuously maintain and update the security posture of all hardware and software throughout its lifecycle.

controls.title: Control Default and Built-In Accounts
controls.description: Disable, rename, or otherwise secure factory-default and built-in accounts on systems and applications.

controls.title: Standardize Application Configuration Management
controls.description: Define and enforce a repeatable process for securely configuring software deployed across the organization.

controls.title: Standardize Network Device Configuration Management
controls.description: Define and enforce a repeatable process for securely configuring routers, switches, firewalls, and other network gear.

controls.title: Require Automatic Lockout on Mobile Devices
controls.description: Enforce policies that automatically lock portable devices after a short period of inactivity or failed authentication attempts.

controls.title: Enable Remote Erase on Mobile Devices
controls.description: Ensure that lost or stolen mobile endpoints can be wiped remotely to prevent data exposure.

controls.title: Separate Work and Personal Data on Mobile Devices
controls.description: Use containerization or profiles to keep organizational data isolated from personal apps and data on mobile endpoints.

implementationGroups.name: Identity and Account Governance
implementationGroups.description: Provision, track, and deprovision credentials and access for human users, administrators, and automated service accounts across all systems and applications.

controls.title: Maintain a Full Account Registry
controls.description: Keep a centralized list of every user, admin, and service account that exists within the organizational environment.

controls.title: Prevent Password Reuse Across Accounts
controls.description: Ensure each account uses a distinct credential so that compromise of one account does not expose others.

controls.title: Disable Inactive Accounts
controls.description: Automatically or manually disable accounts that show no sign-in activity within a defined inactivity window.

controls.title: Limit Admin Rights to Dedicated Accounts
controls.description: Reserve privileged access for accounts specifically created for administrative tasks rather than granting elevation to everyday user accounts.

controls.title: Inventory All Service Accounts
controls.description: Catalog every non-human account used by applications, scripts, or automation so that ownership and purpose are documented.

controls.title: Unify Authentication Across Systems
controls.description: Route authentication through a centralized identity provider rather than maintaining separate credential stores per system.

controls.title: Mandate Strong Password Policies
controls.description: Enforce minimum length, complexity, and rotation rules that make brute-force and guessing attacks impractical.

implementationGroups.name: Access Provisioning and Revocation
implementationGroups.description: Define, grant, monitor, and revoke access rights to systems and data so that users receive only the permissions they need and lose them when no longer appropriate.

controls.title: Define a Formal Access Provisioning Workflow
controls.description: Implement a process — preferably automated — that grants appropriate permissions when a new account is created or a role changes.

controls.title: Define a Formal Access Revocation Workflow
controls.description: Implement a process — preferably automated — that promptly disables or deletes accounts and their permissions when access is no longer required.

controls.title: Require a Second Factor for Internet-Facing Applications
controls.description: Mandate multi-factor authentication on any application accessible from the public internet.

controls.title: Require a Second Factor for VPN and Remote Access
controls.description: Mandate multi-factor authentication for all users connecting to the internal network from outside the corporate perimeter.

controls.title: Require a Second Factor for Privileged Sessions
controls.description: Mandate multi-factor authentication whenever an administrator or operator logs into a system with elevated rights.

controls.title: Inventory All Identity and Access Systems
controls.description: Maintain a registry of every directory, identity provider, and access management platform in use across the organization.

controls.title: Centralize Permission Decisions
controls.description: Route all authorization checks through a unified access control layer rather than managing permissions independently on each system.

controls.title: Implement Role-Based Access Control
controls.description: Assign permissions to organizational roles rather than to individual users, and map each user to one or more roles.

implementationGroups.name: Ongoing Vulnerability Remediation
implementationGroups.description: Continuously discover, prioritize, and address security weaknesses across all systems to shrink the window attackers can exploit.

controls.title: Operate a Vulnerability Management Program
controls.description: Define and follow a documented process for discovering, evaluating, and tracking security weaknesses across the environment.

controls.title: Prioritize and Track Remediation
controls.description: Create a risk-based process that ranks findings by severity and business impact, then tracks fixes to completion.

controls.title: Automate OS Patching
controls.description: Deploy tooling that applies operating system security updates across all endpoints and servers with minimal manual intervention.

controls.title: Automate Application Patching
controls.description: Deploy tooling that applies security updates to third-party and internally developed applications enterprise-wide.

controls.title: Scan Internal Assets for Vulnerabilities Automatically
controls.description: Schedule and run automated scans against all systems inside the corporate network to identify known weaknesses.

controls.title: Scan Internet-Facing Assets for Vulnerabilities Automatically
controls.description: Schedule and run automated scans against externally visible hosts and services to find exposures visible to attackers.

controls.title: Fix Vulnerabilities According to Risk Priority
controls.description: Address discovered weaknesses on a timeline determined by their severity and the asset's criticality.

implementationGroups.name: Log Collection and Audit
implementationGroups.description: Gather, retain, review, and alert on event records from across the environment to support threat detection, investigation, and recovery.

controls.title: Define Logging Requirements
controls.description: Create and maintain a documented policy specifying what events must be captured, for how long, and in what format.

controls.title: Gather Logs From Every System
controls.description: Configure all servers, workstations, network devices, and applications to forward event records to a central collection point.

controls.title: Provision Sufficient Log Storage
controls.description: Allocate storage capacity that accommodates the required retention period without losing records to rotation or overflow.

controls.title: Synchronize Clocks Across All Systems
controls.description: Use a consistent time source such as NTP so that log timestamps from different systems can be reliably correlated.

controls.title: Capture Detailed Event Records
controls.description: Ensure log entries include the event source, timestamp, affected subject or object, and the action's outcome.

controls.title: Collect DNS Query Logs
controls.description: Record DNS requests made by internal systems to support investigation of potentially malicious domain lookups.

controls.title: Collect HTTP Request Logs
controls.description: Record URLs requested by users and systems to aid detection of phishing, command-and-control traffic, and policy violations.

controls.title: Collect Shell and Command-Line Logs
controls.description: Capture commands executed at the prompt or via scripts to create an audit trail of administrative and system activity.

controls.title: Aggregate Logs in a Central Platform
controls.description: Forward all event records to a centralized logging or SIEM platform for unified search, analysis, and alerting.

controls.title: Retain Logs for a Minimum of 90 Days
controls.description: Store log data in readily accessible form for at least 90 days to allow timely investigation of incidents.

controls.title: Review Logs on a Regular Schedule
controls.description: Periodically examine log data — manually or through automated detection rules — to identify suspicious activity.

controls.title: Collect Logs From External Service Providers
controls.description: Obtain relevant event records from third-party vendors and cloud providers that handle organizational data or traffic.

implementationGroups.name: Browser and Email Security
implementationGroups.description: Reduce the attack surface associated with web browsers and email clients — the primary vectors attackers use to manipulate users into taking harmful actions.

controls.title: Mandate Vendor-Supported Browsers and Mail Clients
controls.description: Ensure that only browser and email software receiving active security updates from the vendor is deployed in the environment.

controls.title: Apply DNS-Based Filtering
controls.description: Route DNS queries through a filtering service that blocks lookups for known malicious or unwanted domains.

controls.title: Enforce URL Filtering at the Network Edge
controls.description: Block or warn on web requests to categories of sites that pose unnecessary risk or violate policy.

controls.title: Restrict Browser Extensions
controls.description: Allow only explicitly approved add-ons and extensions in web browsers to reduce the risk of malicious or unwanted functionality.

controls.title: Enable DMARC to Counter Email Spoofing
controls.description: Publish and enforce DMARC records to prevent attackers from sending fraudulent messages that appear to come from your domains.

controls.title: Block High-Risk File Attachments in Email
controls.description: Filter out file types commonly used to deliver malware, such as executables, scripts, and macro-enabled documents.

controls.title: Operate an Email Spam Filter
controls.description: Deploy and continuously tune anti-spam technology to reduce the volume of unsolicited and potentially harmful messages reaching inboxes.

controls.title: Operate Anti-Phishing Defenses
controls.description: Implement technical controls that detect and block phishing attempts, including link scanning and impersonation analysis.

implementationGroups.name: Malicious Code Defenses
implementationGroups.description: Stop or contain harmful software from being installed, spreading, or executing on organizational systems.

controls.title: Deploy and Maintain Endpoint Anti-Malware
controls.description: Install anti-malware agents on every workstation and server and keep them operational and up to date.

controls.title: Automate Malware Signature Updates
controls.description: Configure anti-malware tools to pull the latest detection signatures automatically rather than relying on manual updates.

controls.title: Disable Auto-Run on Removable Media
controls.description: Turn off automatic execution of content on USB drives and other portable storage to prevent malware from launching on insertion.

controls.title: Schedule Regular Malware Scans
controls.description: Configure anti-malware tools to perform full or targeted scans on endpoints at recurring intervals.

controls.title: Manage Anti-Malware From a Central Console
controls.description: Operate all endpoint anti-malware agents through a unified management platform to ensure consistent policy and visibility.

implementationGroups.name: Backup and Restoration
implementationGroups.description: Ensure that systems and data can be restored to a functional state after corruption, deletion, or hardware failure.

controls.title: Define a Data Restoration Strategy
controls.description: Document the approach, tools, and responsibilities for recovering systems and information after a destructive event.

controls.title: Automate Backup Operations
controls.description: Schedule regular, automated copies of critical systems and data to reduce reliance on manual processes.

controls.title: Apply Security Controls to Backup Data
controls.description: Protect backup copies with encryption, access restrictions, and integrity checks equivalent to those applied to the original data.

controls.title: Maintain an Offline or Air-Gapped Backup Copy
controls.description: Keep at least one set of backups isolated from the production network to guard against ransomware and destructive attacks.

controls.title: Test Restoration Procedures Regularly
controls.description: Periodically perform restore drills to confirm that backups are complete, uncorrupted, and recoverable within acceptable timeframes.

implementationGroups.name: Network Architecture and Operations
implementationGroups.description: Design, implement, and actively maintain the network's configuration and topology to prevent unauthorized access, detect threats, and support reliable operations.

controls.title: Keep Network Infrastructure Patched
controls.description: Apply the latest firmware and security updates to routers, switches, firewalls, and other network devices.

controls.title: Design and Maintain a Secure Network Topology
controls.description: Build network segmentation, zoning, and trust boundaries that limit lateral movement and contain breaches.

controls.title: Administer Network Devices Over Secure Channels
controls.description: Use encrypted and authenticated protocols for all management connections to network infrastructure.

controls.title: Keep Network Diagrams Current
controls.description: Maintain up-to-date architecture drawings that show network segments, interconnections, and key devices.

controls.title: Unify Network Authentication and Auditing
controls.description: Route network access authentication, authorization decisions, and activity logs through centralized services.

controls.title: Use Cryptographically Secure Management Protocols
controls.description: Replace cleartext or legacy protocols with modern, encrypted alternatives for all network management communication.

controls.title: Aggregate Network Event Logs
controls.description: Forward logs from routers, switches, firewalls, and other network gear to a central collection and analysis platform.

controls.title: Use Dedicated Systems for Network Administration
controls.description: Perform network management tasks from specially provisioned workstations or jump hosts rather than from general-purpose endpoints.

implementationGroups.name: Network Defense and Monitoring
implementationGroups.description: Operate detection and prevention capabilities across the network and endpoints to identify and block adversarial activity in real time.

controls.title: Route Security Alerts to a Central Platform
controls.description: Send all security-relevant notifications to a unified console or SIEM to ensure timely visibility and triage.

controls.title: Deploy Host-Based Intrusion Detection
controls.description: Install agents on servers and endpoints that monitor local activity for signs of compromise or policy violations.

controls.title: Deploy Network-Based Intrusion Detection
controls.description: Place sensors on the network that analyze traffic patterns for indicators of attack or reconnaissance.

controls.title: Filter Traffic Between Network Zones
controls.description: Enforce rules at zone boundaries that permit only necessary communication and block everything else.

controls.title: Control Access to Remote Systems
controls.description: Apply authentication, authorization, and logging requirements for any connection to systems located outside the corporate network.

controls.title: Collect Network Flow Records
controls.description: Gather metadata about network connections — source, destination, ports, and volume — to support anomaly detection and investigation.

controls.title: Activate Host Firewalls Everywhere
controls.description: Enable and configure firewall software on every endpoint and server to restrict unnecessary inbound and outbound connections.

controls.title: Filter Spam at the Network Layer
controls.description: Deploy an email gateway or appliance that identifies and quarantines spam and phishing messages before they reach user mailboxes.

controls.title: Inspect and Filter Application-Layer Traffic
controls.description: Use proxies or next-generation firewalls that understand application protocols and can block malicious payloads at layer 7.

controls.title: Deploy Network Intrusion Prevention
controls.description: Place inline sensors on the network that can actively block traffic matching known attack signatures or behavioral profiles.

controls.title: Enforce Port-Level Access Restrictions
controls.description: Use techniques such as 802.1X or MAC-based policies to control which devices may connect to specific network ports.

controls.title: Deploy Endpoint Detection and Response
controls.description: Install EDR agents on endpoints that continuously monitor for suspicious behavior and allow remote investigation and containment.

controls.title: Deploy Network Detection and Response
controls.description: Deploy NDR tooling that applies behavioral analytics and machine learning to network traffic to find threats that evade signature-based detection.

implementationGroups.name: Workforce Security Education
implementationGroups.description: Run a recurring training program that builds security awareness across the organization so employees can recognize and resist common attack techniques.

controls.title: Operate an Ongoing Security Awareness Program
controls.description: Establish a formal, recurring program that teaches all personnel about current threats and organizational security policies.

controls.title: Train Staff to Recognize Manipulation Attempts
controls.description: Educate employees on how to identify phishing emails, pretexting calls, and other social engineering tactics.

controls.title: Train Staff on Credential Hygiene
controls.description: Teach users how to create strong passwords, recognize credential-harvesting sites, and use multi-factor authentication.

controls.title: Train Staff on Secure Data Handling
controls.description: Instruct employees on how to classify, store, share, and dispose of data in accordance with organizational policies.

controls.title: Train Staff on Avoiding Accidental Data Disclosure
controls.description: Help employees understand common mistakes — such as misconfigured cloud storage or auto-forwarding rules — that lead to data exposure.

controls.title: Train Staff on Current Threats and Risk
controls.description: Brief the workforce on relevant threat actors, attack trends, and the potential business impact of security incidents.

controls.title: Train Staff to Spot and Report Missing Patches
controls.description: Show employees how to check whether their devices are current on security updates and how to report gaps to the IT team.

controls.title: Train Staff on Risks of Untrusted Networks
controls.description: Educate users about the dangers of sending organizational data over public Wi-Fi or other unsecured connections.

implementationGroups.name: Vendor and Third-Party Risk Management
implementationGroups.description: Evaluate, contractually bind, and continuously monitor every external party that touches organizational data or systems to ensure they meet the enterprise's security expectations.

controls.title: Catalog All Third-Party Vendors
controls.description: Maintain a registry of every external provider with access to organizational systems or data.

controls.title: Define a Vendor Security Policy
controls.description: Create and enforce a policy that specifies the minimum security requirements all service providers must satisfy.

controls.title: Classify Vendors by Risk Level
controls.description: Categorize each vendor according to the sensitivity of the data they handle and the criticality of the services they provide.

controls.title: Embed Security Clauses in Vendor Contracts
controls.description: Include enforceable security requirements, audit rights, and breach notification obligations in all service provider agreements.

controls.title: Assess Vendor Security Posture Regularly
controls.description: Periodically evaluate each provider's security practices through questionnaires, audits, or third-party ratings.

controls.title: Monitor Vendors on an Ongoing Basis
controls.description: Continuously watch for changes in vendor risk — such as security incidents, ownership changes, or compliance failures — and act on them.

controls.title: Revoke Vendor Access When No Longer Needed
controls.description: Promptly remove credentials, VPN access, API keys, and data shares when a vendor relationship ends.

implementationGroups.name: Application Security Lifecycle
implementationGroups.description: Integrate security into every phase of the software development lifecycle — from design through deployment — to reduce the number of vulnerabilities shipped to production.

controls.title: Track All Applications in a Security Inventory
controls.description: Maintain a registry of every application — internal, custom, and third-party — that identifies ownership and security status.

controls.title: Define an Application Security Policy
controls.description: Create and enforce standards covering secure coding, testing requirements, and acceptable risk for application development.

controls.title: Perform Root Cause Analysis on Defects
controls.description: When a vulnerability is found, investigate the underlying process or design flaw that allowed it and take corrective action.

controls.title: Harden Application Infrastructure
controls.description: Define and apply secure configuration baselines to web servers, containers, databases, and other platforms hosting applications.

controls.title: Use Current, Trusted Third-Party Dependencies
controls.description: Prefer actively maintained libraries and frameworks from reputable sources, and update them promptly when fixes are released.

controls.title: Operate a Vulnerability Disclosure Process
controls.description: Establish a channel through which external researchers can report security issues and define how those reports are triaged and resolved.

controls.title: Evaluate Third-Party Software Before Adoption
controls.description: Assess the security posture of vendor-supplied and open-source software before integrating it into the environment.

controls.title: Measure Application Security Maturity
controls.description: Define metrics and periodically evaluate how effectively the organization identifies, prevents, and remediates software weaknesses.

controls.title: Apply Hardening Templates to Application Platforms
controls.description: Use recognized configuration guides — such as CIS benchmarks — to lock down the infrastructure that runs applications.

controls.title: Embed Security Into Application Architecture
controls.description: Design applications with principles like least privilege, defense in depth, and secure defaults from the outset.

controls.title: Use Vetted Libraries for Security Functions
controls.description: Prefer well-reviewed, established modules for cryptography, authentication, and other security-critical operations rather than building custom implementations.

controls.title: Integrate Security Checks Into the Build Pipeline
controls.description: Run static analysis, dependency scans, and other automated security tests as part of the continuous integration process.

controls.title: Conduct Application Penetration Tests
controls.description: Hire qualified testers or use skilled internal teams to probe applications for exploitable weaknesses before and after release.

controls.title: Provide Security Training to Developers
controls.description: Educate software engineers on secure coding practices, common vulnerability classes, and how to use security testing tools.

implementationGroups.name: Incident Handling and Response
implementationGroups.description: Build and maintain the people, processes, and tools needed to detect, contain, investigate, and recover from security incidents effectively.

controls.title: Assign Incident Response Ownership
controls.description: Designate individuals or a team responsible for coordinating the organization's response to security events.

controls.title: Publish Reporting Contacts for Security Events
controls.description: Maintain and distribute up-to-date contact details so that anyone who observes suspicious activity knows whom to notify.

controls.title: Maintain an Enterprise Incident Management Plan
controls.description: Create a comprehensive plan that defines how the organization prepares for, detects, and recovers from security incidents.

controls.title: Define and Follow an Incident Response Process
controls.description: Document step-by-step procedures — from initial triage through containment, eradication, and recovery — and adhere to them during every incident.

controls.title: Clarify Roles and Responsibilities During Incidents
controls.description: Assign specific duties — such as communications lead, technical lead, and executive sponsor — so that response efforts are coordinated.

controls.title: Establish Incident Communication Channels
controls.description: Pre-define how responders share information during an event, including secure out-of-band channels and escalation paths.

controls.title: Run Regular Incident Drills
controls.description: Conduct tabletop or live exercises periodically to test the response plan and improve team readiness.

controls.title: Perform Post-Incident Reviews
controls.description: After each significant event, analyze what happened, what worked, what failed, and what improvements are needed.

controls.title: Define Criteria for Escalation
controls.description: Establish thresholds — such as the number of affected systems or data sensitivity — that determine when an event is elevated to a formal incident.

implementationGroups.name: Offensive Security Testing
implementationGroups.description: Simulate real-world attacks against systems, networks, and applications to validate that defenses are effective and to find weaknesses before adversaries do.

controls.title: Operate a Formal Offensive Testing Program
controls.description: Establish a structured, repeatable program that defines scope, frequency, rules of engagement, and remediation tracking for penetration tests.

controls.title: Conduct Internal Network Penetration Tests
controls.description: Periodically test systems inside the corporate perimeter from an attacker's perspective to find exploitable weaknesses.

controls.title: Remediate Issues Found During Testing
controls.description: Track every finding from penetration tests to resolution, verifying that fixes are applied and effective.

controls.title: Conduct External Network Penetration Tests
controls.description: Periodically test internet-facing systems and services to identify exposures that an outside attacker could exploit.

controls.title: Use Penetration Tests to Validate Defenses
controls.description: Design tests specifically to confirm that security controls — such as segmentation, monitoring, and access restrictions — work as intended.

controls.title: Combine Manual and Automated Testing Approaches
controls.description: Use both scripted scanners and human testers to ensure broad coverage and the ability to find logic flaws that tools miss.

controls.title: Assess and Penetration-Test Internet-Facing Systems
controls.description: Run vulnerability scans and targeted exploitation attempts against externally visible hosts and services.

controls.title: Penetration-Test Internal Systems
controls.description: Evaluate the security of systems on the internal network by attempting to gain unauthorized access or elevate privileges.

controls.title: Penetration-Test Web Applications
controls.description: Subject web-facing applications to structured attacks — including injection, authentication bypass, and business-logic abuse — to find exploitable flaws.

controls.title: Penetration-Test Network Infrastructure
controls.description: Target routers, switches, firewalls, and other network gear with attack simulations to identify configuration weaknesses.
